req.title: Procedure record (audit)
req.statement: A clinician shall record a Procedure tied to an Encounter. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-086 mandates three verifiable concerns: (1) a Procedure linked to a valid Encounter is persisted (positive/EP), (2) a Procedure submitted without an Encounter reference is rejected with HTTP 422 (negative/NEG), and (3) an AuditEvent is emitted for every operation—tested at the boundary where exactly one operation produces exactly one event (boundary/BVA). A fourth case covers the state-transition from 'encounter open' to 'procedure recorded' (STC). Together these satisfy the minimum suite requirements and trace directly to the requirement statement.

tests.name: test_create_procedure_with_valid_encounter_returns_201
preconditions: FHIR-lite service is running and reachable
A valid Encounter resource exists (encounter_id fixture)
Clinician auth token is available (auth_headers fixture)
actions: Build a Procedure payload referencing the open Encounter
POST /Procedure with valid payload and auth headers
Capture HTTP status code and response body
expected: Response body contains encounter reference matching fixture encounter_id
Response body contains a server-assigned id
trace: R-HLT-086: clinician records Procedure tied to Encounter
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_procedure_with_valid_encounter_returns_201(
    base_url, auth_headers, open_encounter_id
):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Procedure",
        "status": "completed",
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-1"},
        "encounter": {"reference": f"Encounter/{open_encounter_id}"},
        "code": {
            "coding": [{"system": "http://snomed.info/sct",
                        "code": "80146002",
                        "display": "Appendectomy"}]
        }
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Procedure",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, resp.text
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["resourceType"] == "Procedure"
    assert body["encounter"]["reference"] == f"Encounter/{open_encounter_id}"
    assert "id" in body

tests.name: test_create_procedure_without_encounter_returns_422
preconditions: FHIR-lite service is running and reachable
Clinician auth token is available (auth_headers fixture)
No encounter reference is included in the payload
actions: Build a Procedure payload that omits the encounter field entirely
POST /Procedure with the incomplete payload and auth headers
Capture HTTP status code and response body
expected: Response body contains an OperationOutcome or error message indicating missing encounter
trace: R-HLT-086: error pathway — 422 when encounter missing

tests.name: test_single_procedure_creation_emits_exactly_one_audit_event
preconditions: FHIR-lite service is running and reachable
A valid Encounter resource exists (open_encounter_id fixture)
Clinician auth token is available (auth_headers fixture)
actions: Record baseline AuditEvent count for this entity before the operation
POST one Procedure payload referencing the open Encounter
Query /AuditEvent filtering by the new Procedure id
Assert exactly one new AuditEvent exists for the create operation
expected: Exactly 1 AuditEvent returned for the created Procedure id
AuditEvent action field indicates 'C' (create) or equivalent
trace: R-HLT-086: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation
BVA: boundary of count == 1 (minimum non-zero audit record)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_single_procedure_creation_emits_exactly_one_audit_event(
    base_url, auth_headers, open_encounter_id
):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Procedure",
        "status": "in-progress",
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-2"},
        "encounter": {"reference": f"Encounter/{open_encounter_id}"},
        "code": {
            "coding": [{"system": "http://snomed.info/sct",
                        "code": "173171007",
                        "display": "Cholecystectomy"}]
        }
    }
    create_resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Procedure",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert create_resp.status_code == 201, create_resp.text
    procedure_id = create_resp.json()["id"]

    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": f"Procedure/{procedure_id}"},
        headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200, audit_resp.text
    entries = audit_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) == 1, (
        f"Expected exactly 1 AuditEvent, got {len(entries)}"
    )
    action = entries[0]["resource"].get("action")
    assert action in ("C", "create"), f"Unexpected action: {action}"

tests.name: test_procedure_state_transition_open_encounter_to_recorded
preconditions: FHIR-lite service is running and reachable
An Encounter in status 'in-progress' exists (open_encounter_id fixture)
Clinician auth token is available (auth_headers fixture)
actions: Verify the Encounter is in state 'in-progress' via GET /Encounter/{id}
POST a Procedure with status 'completed' referencing that Encounter
GET the created Procedure and verify its status is 'completed'
Confirm the Encounter reference is preserved in the stored Procedure
expected: Encounter status is 'in-progress' before Procedure creation
Stored Procedure encounter reference matches open_encounter_id
trace: R-HLT-086: precondition — encounter open or referenced
STC: state transition from 'encounter open' -> 'procedure recorded'
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_procedure_state_transition_open_encounter_to_recorded(
    base_url, auth_headers, open_encounter_id
):
    enc_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/Encounter/{open_encounter_id}",
        headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert enc_resp.status_code == 200
    assert enc_resp.json()["status"] == "in-progress"

    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Procedure",
        "status": "completed",
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-3"},
        "encounter": {"reference": f"Encounter/{open_encounter_id}"},
        "code": {
            "coding": [{"system": "http://snomed.info/sct",
                        "code": "44946007",
                        "display": "Suturing of wound"}]
        }
    }
    post_resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Procedure", json=payload, headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert post_resp.status_code == 201, post_resp.text
    proc_id = post_resp.json()["id"]

    get_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/Procedure/{proc_id}", headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    stored = get_resp.json()
    assert stored["status"] == "completed"
    assert stored["encounter"]["reference"] == f"Encounter/{open_encounter_id}"
